page: Amazon Web Services r5d.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs, corresponding to 16 physical cores with hyperthreading, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration is backed by 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. Storage is bundled as 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD, and the instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% on stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Conversely, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle-50% scores in multi-threaded stress-ng, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only large block speeds reaching 110.87 GB/sec.

This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory density and fast local scratch space, without needing top-tier single-threaded compute. The combination of 256 GB RAM and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD makes it suitable for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive data processing. It is less suited for large-scale LLM inference, where large model prompt processing falls into the bottom 10% of benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Benchmark results reveal a tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth achieve average, middle-50% marks. The instance performs reliably on database operations, Redis SET operations, and medium-sized language model inference. With its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage, the r5d.8xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications, caching layers, and database workloads that benefit from local NVMe scratch space, though it is not optimal for single-threaded compute or large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 RAM
1200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Weak single-core CPU and large-scale LLM inference performance
Well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive tasks
